title: pyrus maliformis
intro: Pyrus maliformis, also known as the eastern sweet sharon or the Malus domestica, is a species of deciduous tree or shrub that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The plant is primarily valued for its edible fruits, which are rich in medicinal properties, containing tannins, flavonoids, and anthocyanins. The fruits of Pyrus maliformis have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, diarrhea, and inflammation, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is often prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
